title: agrostis gigantea subsp. gautieri
intro: Agrostis gigantea subsp. gautieri, also known as alpine bent or gautier's bent, is a subspecies of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Agrostis gigantea subsp. gautieri include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Agrostis gigantea subsp. gautieri can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
